aboutUsDescription1: INZO is a leading global online trading provider, offering trading with Forex and CFDs, indices, precious metals, energy and cryptocurrencies. Our strong dedication to state-of-the-art software and quality assurance means that our clients receive the best, most transparent trading experience.
aboutUsDescription2: Our company is authorized to provide financial services to clients around the world except for United States of America, Canada, Israel and the Islamic Republic of Iran.
aboutUsDescription3: Suite 305, Griffith Corporate Centre, Beachmont, Kingstown, St. Vincent and Grenadines.
aboutUsDescription4: INZO does not target its website and services to any individual in any country where the local laws and regulations would object the user from using INZO's website or services. When entering the Website from a country, whether or not such use is prohibited, the user is responsible for ensuring that any use of the website or the Services complies with the local laws and regulations. INZO does not confirm that the information on the website is appropriate for all countries or jurisdictions.
aboutUsDescription5: The main message and mission that INZO seeks to achieve tirelessly is to create a trading environment that enjoys the highest levels of efficiency and transparency to serve both individual and institutional clients in a way that allows them to focus their efforts on trading without being preoccupied with any other matters. INZO was founded by traders and for serving traders, for this reason, INZO has tried from the first moment to provide the lowest spread rates, the best services, and high-speed execution.
financialInstrumentsDescription: INZO offers more than {TRADING_TOOLS} tradable financial instruments, trade now forex, stocks, energy, metals, and CFDs.
platformsTitle3: Advantages of MT5 in Online Trading
platformsText1_1: The online trading platform MetaTrader 5, or commonly known as MT5, is the successor of the well-known MetaTrader 4 (MT4). It was developed by MetaQuotes Software Corp., and initially released in 2010 as a beta version. Over the years, MetaQuotes had invested in continuous development, testing and improvements, which made MT5 today as the next generation of trading platforms with extended possibilities.
platformsText1_2: The biggest advantage that MT5 possesses over its predecessor is its flexibility to offer an extended number of instruments from various asset classes with different settings, all tradable from the same interface and the same trading account.
platformsText1_3: MetaTrader 5 (MT5) takes the famous charting package and the automated trading possibilities to a whole new level. All the features that made MetaTrader 4 (MT4) the platform of choice have been kept and have been upgraded to a more sophisticated back end infrastructure that can support trading on multiple instruments from various asset classes.
platformsText2_1: MT5 gives online investors the option to trade on multiple asset classes ranging from forex (currency trading), Contracts for Difference (CFDs) for individual stocks (shares), stock indices, precious metals, energies and commodities.
platformsText2_2: Whether for beginner, more advanced or professional online traders, MT5 has opened up new perspectives to not just trade but also gain better visibility across different markets and different asset classes.
platformsText2_3: Moreover, in the 21st century, the ability for online investors to trade various financial assets from one account offers the benefit of strategic asset allocation (or asset management), with the primary aim to allocate the invested capital in several different assets and manage risk according to market volatility, as well as according to how those assets perform in the short or in the long run.
platformsText3_1: The multi-functionality of the MT5 platform includes powerful features, which were all developed with state-of-the-art technology and by keeping several major criteria in mind, the most important ones being the same second to none charting package, the possibilities and flexibility of automated trading (algorithmic trading) and now a diversity of markets supported.
platformsText3_2: For both beginner and professional traders MT5 ensures platform features and functionalities that are in line with the highest expectations of any online investor today. These include the variety of pending order types (buy limit, buy stop, sell limit, sell stop, buy stop limit, sell stop limit); a database that holds an unlimited number of trade positions that can be placed and the cutting-edge programming language MQL5 that allows traders to program their own trading robots and indicators customized to their individual trading preferences and choice of financial assets to trade on.
platformsText4_1: The MT5 suite comes with apps for both iOS and Android devices that offer full access to your account while on the go, with features including the unrivalled mobile chart plus technical analysis options with over 30 built-in indicators and 24 analytical objects.
platformsCTraderTitle3: The advantages of cTrader in online
platformsCTraderText1_1: C-Trader is a major and independent trading platform launched in 2011 by Spotware Systems Ltd. It is based on the ECN/STP system that guarantees fairness and transparency in pricing and execution. It is used by a number of large forex brokers and users can choose a certified broker such as Inzo to open a live or demo account on this platform.
platformsCTraderText1_2: They can take advantage of cTrader's features on their favorite devices without the intervention of any trading desks. The platform is fast and efficient in executing deals and providing a clear view of price movement and market news.
platformsCTraderText1_3: Using cTrader with Inzo provides traders with many benefits, as it provides a well-organized and advanced trading platform, gives traders access to technical analysis tools and real-time economic news, and also offers tools to manage risks and control trades with ease. In addition, Inzo is distinguished for its excellent customer service, which helps traders to quickly and effectively solve any problem they encounter. Therefore, it can be said that using cTrader with Inzo is a good option for those who are looking for an excellent and efficient trading experience.
platformsCTraderText2_1: A multi-asset trading platform aimed at both beginners and professional traders, designed to provide a transparent, fast and reliable trading experience suitable for the new, technologically advanced trading market.
platformsCTraderText2_2: Featuring an intuitive and easy-to-use user interface, the platform gives you access to over 150 currency pairs and over 250 stocks, indices and commodities through Inzo.
platformsCTraderText2_3: And the benefits of the platform are not limited to this, as it includes a wide range of indicators and graphical tools to aid in technical analysis, in addition to custom options for layout, colors and styles.
platformsCTraderText3_1: cTrader is an online trading platform with advanced features that caters to both beginners and professional traders. The platform offers an easy and intuitive user interface, algorithmic trading, advanced charting and market depth display features, which aids in technical analysis and the development of trading strategies.
platformsCTraderText3_2: Basic trading features are also available such as pending and instant orders, financial risk management tools, pre-order protection and a comprehensive time frame ranging from 45, 20, 10 up to 1 hour time frame...etc. It has advanced trading capabilities, fast entry, synchronization and fast order processing. With the Market Sentiment indicator, the overall sentiment of each trader at a particular point in time is shown. cTrader publishes the deals directly in the market, and takes a reasonable percentage of the profit for the service provided by the brokers (commission $1 per lot of 1.00 standard). In addition, novice traders can benefit from the functionality and features of this platform but it may be more suitable for experienced traders
platformsCTraderText4_1: cTrader allows users to trade on iOS and Android mobile phones. The cTrader application can be downloaded to smartphones and traded anytime and anywhere. Full trading features are provided on the mobile app, as well as unified login credentials: since the infrastructure for its cTrader platform is run by Spotware, the user gets a single username, email or ID that they use to log into all their trading accounts on the platform (i.e. all their accounts can be accessed). To be managed and shown from one Gmail) and after he enters the first time, the data is saved and the user will not have to remember passwords, account numbers, etc. - he can also move between the brokers he works with without exiting and entering again
forexTermsDescription: There are many important terms that you should know as a Forex trader
forexTermsText1_2: The spread is the difference between the buy (ask) and the sell (bid) price of an instrument.
forexTermsText1_3: The spread is one of the chief costs of a trade
forexTermsText1_4: The tighter (smaller) the spread, the lower the cost of the trade
forexTermsText1_5: We offer both ‘fixed’ spreads and ‘floating’ spreads, so you can decide which account best suits your trading style and strategy
forexTermsText1_6: The spread on financial markets is the difference between the buy (ask) price of an instrument and the sell (bid) price of an instrument. When placing a trade on the market, the spread is also the main cost of the position. The tighter the spread, the lower the cost of trading. The wider the spread, the higher it costs. You can also view the spread as the minimum distance the market has to move in your favour before you could start earning a profit.
forexTermsText1_7: For example, let’s say our EURUSD market is quoted with a buy price of 1.0984 and a sell price of 1.0983, so the spread is calculated by subtracting 1.0983 from 1.0984 – giving a total spread of 0.0001 or 1 pip. Once you’ve placed a trade on the EURUSD market and the market moves at least 1 pip in your favour, that’s when your position can begin generating profits. This is also the reason that when you first place the trade, you’ll start off making a small loss.
forexTermsText2_2: A currency pair is made of up two currencies traded in the forex market
forexTermsText2_3: All currency pairs are quoted in terms of one currency versus another
forexTermsText2_4: Each currency pair has a ‘base’, which is the first denoted currency, and a ‘counter’ which is the second denoted currency
forexTermsText2_5: Each currency could strengthen (appreciate) or weaken (depreciate). As there are two currencies in each pair, there are essentially four variables you are speculating on when it comes to currency pairs
forexTermsText2_6: There are major currency pairs and minor/exotic currency pairs
forexTermsText2_7: All forex pairs are quoted in terms of one currency versus another. Each currency pair has a base which is the first denoted currency, and a counter which is the second.
forexTermsText2_8: When you place a trade on a currency pair, you’re essentially buying one currency and selling another – but in a single transaction. So for example, going long or ‘buying’ EUR/USD means you’re buying the Euro and selling the US dollar. Going short means that you’re ‘selling’ the Euro and buying the US dollar.
forexTermsText2_9: Currency values rise (appreciate) and fall (depreciate) against each other due to a number of economic, geopolitical and technical factors and the forex market is the most traded in the world, with an average turnover in excess of $5 trillion a day. This makes it a highly volatile market and it’s available to trade on 24 hours a day, five days a week (Monday to Friday).
forexTermsText2_10: What are the major currency pairs?
forexTermsText2_11: Major currency pairs are the most traded currency pairs in the world and it is estimated that trading on those currencies represents over 80% of the whole foreign exchange market. Those currency pairs are: EURUSD, GBPUSD, USDCHF, AUDUSD, NZDUSD and USDCAD.
forexTermsText2_12: Out of all the majors, the EURUSD is the most liquid currency pair; meaning that it is the most traded currency pair in the world.
forexTermsText3_2: Contracts For Difference (CFDs) are specialized and popular Over The Counter (OTC) financial derivative products which enable you to trade on the price movement of financial assets, including Indices Futures, Commodity Futures, Shares, and Exchange Traded Funds, without actually owning the underlying Asset.The main benefit of trading CFDs is the flexibility to trade against price movements without actually buying or selling the physical financial assets. INZO’s CFDs derive their price from the underlying asset. You can trade CFDs if you believe the price of a financial instrument is likely to go up in value (strengthen), and also if you think it is likely to go down (weaken). Your profit or loss in online CFD trading is determined by the difference between the price you buy at and the price at which you sell.
forexTermsText4_2: Leverage allows a client to trade without putting up the full amount. Instead a margin amount is required. For example, 50:1 leverage, also known as 2% margin requirement, means $2,000 of equity is required to purchase an order worth $100,000. 400:1 leverage means $250 is required to purchase an order worth $100,000. Leverage increases both upside and downside to risk as the account is now that much more sensitive to price movements.
forexTermsText5_1: A margin call is a protective measure that helps traders to manage their risk and prevent additional losses.
forexTermsText5_2: A margin call, also known as a margin stop, is a protective measure that helps traders to manage their risk and prevent additional losses
forexTermsText5_3: The limit level is calculated by dividing your equity by the required margin and multiplying by 100%
forexTermsText5_4: Margin calls happen when there aren’t sufficient funds to cover the margin requirement
forexTermsText5_5: At INZO, a margin call occurs when your margin level falls below 30%
forexTermsText5_6: Once a margin call occurs, your open position with the biggest loss will be automatically closed until your margin level returns back above 20% to protect your account from suffering further losses. This is how INZO helps you to manage your risk.
forexTermsText5_7: To avoid being closed out of your position by a margin call, you’ll need to ensure your margin level remains above 20% by depositing more funds
forexTermsText5_8: Limiting losses is one of the most important aspects of trading and many traders choose to use stop loss orders (hyperlink to stop loss) as a protective measure. On the other hand, some traders decide to manage their risk manually by monitoring their open transactions.
forexTermsText5_9: Your margin level is the deposit required to maintain each open trade on your account. To open and maintain your trade, you must have sufficient trading resource to cover the margin requirement at all time.
forexTermsText5_10: Free margin represents the amount of capital you have remaining to place new trades or cover any negative price moves in your open trades.
forexTermsText5_11: The margin stop is a protective measure, particularly for traders who do not use stop loss orders. When the margin level falls below 30%, your open position with the biggest loss will be automatically closed as an in-built safety mechanism.
forexTermsText6_1: What is the margin level and how does it affect your trading?
forexTermsText6_2: The margin level is a risk management indicator that helps you understand the influence of the currently opened positions on your account.
forexTermsText6_3: Margin level is a mathematical equation that effectively tells the trader how much of their funds are available for new trades
forexTermsText6_4: The higher the margin level, the higher the amount of cash available to trade
forexTermsText6_5: The lower the margin level, the lower the amount of cash available to trade and this is where an account could be subject to a margin call
forexTermsText6_7: It is calculated by the following formula:
forexTermsText6_9: If you don’t have any trades open, your margin level will be zero. Once a position is opened, the margin level will depend on several factors such as:
forexTermsText7_2: Rollovers on financial instruments occur overnight on the underlying instrument.
forexTermsText7_3: They affect traders by accruing or charging open positions with either positive or negative swap points, depending on the instrument.
forexTermsText7_4: To maintain a trade or rollover the trade from one day to the next, the trade is subject to a swap. This is otherwise known as the amount of interest charged or credited to your account to maintain the trade and differs across instruments.
forexTermsText7_5: In order to have a clear picture about how a trader’s position may be affected by holding the position overnight, it is important to understand how many pips will be accrued or charged on the trader’s position.
forexTermsText7_6: Swap points may be calculated manually after calculating the value of a pip.
forexTermsText7_7: If a trader opens a 1 lot transaction on the EURUSD, then if the trader holds a buy transaction overnight his account would be accrued with the value of 0.05 GBP. On the other hand if the trader holds a sell transaction overnight his account would be charged with the value of 3.79 GBP.
forexTermsText7_8: The accruals and the charges are dependant on the financial instrument the trader is interested in and many traders have strategies based on holding positions overnight in order to receive the highest possible accruals. Popular markets used for such strategies include USDTRY, USDMXN and EURAUD.
forexTermsText7_9: Additionally most indices do not cause any accruals or charges to the trader’s open positions and you can find the complete swap point rates
forexTermsText7_11: The swap point rates may help traders to manually calculate the swap points that may affect open positions and this can be done by the following calculation: Swap point rate x Pip value.
forexTermsText8_2: There are two transactions types; instant execution and pending orders.
forexTermsText8_3: Whereas instant execution opens a transaction at the price currently quoted on the market, pending orders allow you to set orders that will be activated once the price reaches a level chosen by you.
forexTermsText8_4: There are four types of pending orders that you can utilise at INZO and they include:
forexTermsText8_6: The Buy Stop order allows you to set a buy order above the current market price. What this means is that if the current market price is $20 and the set Buy Stop price is $22, then once the market reaches the price level of $22, a buy transaction will be executed on this market.
forexTermsText8_8: The Sell Stop order allows you to set a sell order below the current market price. What this means is that if the current market price is $20 and the set Sell Stop price is $18, then once the market reaches the price level of $18, a sell transaction will be executed on this market.
forexTermsText8_10: The Buy Limit order allows you to set a buy order below the current market price. What this means is that if the current market price is $20 and the set Buy Limit price is $18, then once the market reaches the price level of $18, a buy transaction will be executed on this market.
forexTermsText8_12: The Sell Limit order allows you to set a sell order above the current market price. What this means is that if the current market price is $20 and the set Sell Limit price is $22, then once the market reaches the price level of $22, a sell transaction will be executed on this market.
forexTermsText9_2: A Stop Loss is a type of closing order to automatically close a trade once prices hit a specific level in the market, normally for a loss
forexTermsText9_3: A Stop Loss is one of the most popular tools for traders to minimise their risk
forexTermsText9_4: If you are only willing to accept a certain level of loss, a Stop Loss will close the trade once prices hit the the price level you’ve defined.
forexTermsText9_5: A Stop Loss is automatic – so you don’t have to manually monitor your positions. This provides a certain level of control and comfort
forexTermsText9_6: Experienced traders will testify that one of the keys to achieving success on financial markets over the long term is prudent risk management. Utilising a stop loss is one of the most popular ways for a trader to manage their risk, around the clock.
forexTermsText9_7: What is a Stop Loss order?
forexTermsText9_8: A stop loss is a type of closing order, allowing the trader to specify a specific level in the market where if prices were to hit, the trade would be closed out by our systems automatically, typically for a loss. This is where the name Stop Loss originates from, because the order effectively stops your losses.
forexTermsText9_9: How does a Stop Loss order work in practice?
forexTermsText9_10: Let’s analyse the below example. The trader has opened a long position on EURUSD in expectation that it will increase in value above 1.09935, which is shown by the first line. You’ll notice a second line below that, which is a Stop Loss set at 1.09842. This means that if the market falls beneath this level, the trader’s position will be automatically closed at a loss – and therefore the trader is protected from any additional  price moves lower.
forexTermsText9_11: A Stop Loss helps to manage your risk and keep your losses to an acceptable and controlled minimum amount.
forexTermsText9_12: Do stop losses provide complete protection?
forexTermsText9_13: Whilst stop loss orders are one of the best ways to ensure your risk is managed and potential losses are kept to acceptable levels, they don’t provide 100% security.
forexTermsText9_14: Stop losses are free to use and they protect your account against adverse market moves, but please be aware that they cannot guarantee your position every time. If the market becomes suddenly volatile and gaps beyond your stop level (jumps from one price to the next without trading at the levels in between), it’s possible your position could be closed at a worse level than requested. This is known as price slippage.
forexTermsText10_2: It’s the experience of not getting filled at (or even very close to…) your expected price when you place a market order or stop loss. This can happen because either: market price is simply moving too fast, the market is not liquid or you’re talking to an unmotivated broker. Slippage typically occurs in a very volatile market (such as when there is a systemic effect that causes the entire market, asset or currency to fall, creating a situation where there are no traders to purchase the position at a stop loss. Sometimes, it occurs because brokers fill the large trade orders of institutional traders first, and by the time they move to the smaller retail orders, the large demand on the asset created by the institutional orders has driven prices too far.
forexTermsText11_2: The smallest increment of change in a foreign currency price, either up or down. A pip is known as percentage interest point, and is equivalent to 0.0001 or ten-thousandths of a unit number of 1. For the Yen crosses, a pip is equivalent to 0.01
forexTermsText12_2: The price at which the market is prepared to buy a product. Prices are quoted two-way as Bid/Ask. In FX trading, the Bid represents the price at which a trader can sell the base currency, shown to the left in a currency pair. For example, in the quote USD/CHF 1.4527/32, the base currency is USD, and the Bid price is 1.4527, meaning you can sell one US Dollar for 1.4527 Swiss francs. In CFD trading, the Bid also represents the price at which a trader can sell the product. For example, in the quote for UK OIL 111.13/111.16, the Bid price is £111.13 for one unit of the underlying market
forexTermsText13_2: The price at which the market is prepared to sell a product. Prices are quoted two-way as Bid/Ask. The Ask price is also known as the Offer.
forexTermsText13_3: In FX trading, the Ask represents the price at which a trader can buy the base currency, shown to the left in a currency pair. For example, in the quote USD/CHF 1.4527/32, the base currency is USD, and the Ask price is 1.4532, meaning you can buy one US dollar for 1.4532 Swiss francs.
forexTermsText13_4: In CFD trading, the Ask also represents the price at which a trader can buy the product. For example, in the quote for UK OIL 111.13/111.16, the product quoted is UK OIL and the Ask price is £111.16 for one unit of the underlying market.
forexTermsText14_2: the secured part of the client account, including open positions, that is bound to the Balance and the Floating rate (profit/loss) by the following formula: Balance + Floating + Swap, i.e. the funds on the client account minus the current loss of the open positions, plus the current profit of the open positions.

ibProgramDescription1: Introducing Brokers (IBs) are companies or individuals across the globe who make money by introducing clients to INZO . We partner with our IBs in an effort to achieve success by offering them one of the best IB commission rates among Forex brokers. Contact us to learn more about our offer.
ibProgramDescription2: Our IB program is not limited to companies or individuals with large lists of potential clients – every trade generated by your referral will earn you money.
ibProgramDescription3: Introducing INZO  is effortless – our intuitive IB Room offers you advanced fund and account management tools as well as a set of ready-made advertising materials to effectively attract new clients.
ibBox1Description: Becoming our IB is very simple – no paperwork, no costs and it takes only 30 seconds of your time to kick-start.
ibBox2Description: Everything is ready for you to promote us – links, logos, banners, landing pages. Mix and match them the way you like.
ibBox3Description: Get commission up to $7 per lot on your Standard account from each trade done by your customer
investPammBox1Description: Percentage allocation management module, also known as percentage allocation money management or PAMM, is a form of pooled money forex trading. An investor gets to allocate his or her money in desired proportion to the qualified trader(s)/money manager(s) of his or her choice. These traders/managers may manage multiple forex trading accounts using their own capital and such pooled moneys, with an aim to generate profits.
investPammBox2Description: Aggregate Portfolio Management Service – PAMM offers the ability to join the largest aggregate investment interface in the Forex market as professional financial managers through which investors can monitor performance, trading results and investment request directly with the appropriate CFO.

stpDescription: It is a system adopted by companies that direct clients’ trades to a group of liquidity providers. This system works according to tools that are not used by the trading platform provider, but rather by a third party that connects a group of liquidity providers with a system called (Aggregation), where this system works to purify the received prices from liquidity providers and obtain the best price for buying and the best price for selling, for example, as the purchase price can be from the X liquidity provider, and the selling price from the Y liquidity provider. Companies prefer this system to obtain the best price differences to provide to their customers, as this system provides price differences up to (- 3) Inzo adopts this system through the (Aggregation) system provided by OneZero and Centroid, which are software companies (third parties) that provide solutions to link trades between the trading platform provided by the brokerage company and liquidity providers.

qaBonusA1: Inzo offers a welcome bonus of $30 under the following conditions:
qaBonusA1_1: Traders can trade the bonus as a regular deposit. Bonus funds support your margin during trading but cannot be withdrawn.
qaBonusA1_2: Bonus funds can be added only to a Standard account type.
qaBonusA1_3: The welcome bonus is granted exclusively to one family member (mother, father, husband, wife, sister, brother, son, daughter) and only to those with valid documents. No other member is allowed to claim it.
qaBonusA1_4: The welcome bonus is granted to one account only and only once.
qaBonusA1_5: Profits can be withdrawn after reaching $70 in your balance. During the withdrawal process, the bonus returns to the company.
qaBonusA1_6: Upon adding the welcome bonus, it must be traded. If the bonus is not traded within 30 days, it will be automatically withdrawn.
qaBonusA1_7: The bonus cannot be transferred between sub-accounts.
qaBonusA1_8: The welcome bonus offer is available only to residents and inhabitants of Arab countries.
qaBonusA1_8_1: The welcome bonus is granted only to clients under the age of 50.
qaBonusA1_9: Proof of residency is required for some countries to be eligible for the welcome bonus.
qaBonusA1_10: The welcome bonus is traded with a leverage of 1:100. When the client makes a deposit, we convert the leverage to 1:500.
qaBonusA2: The welcome bonus offer is available only to residents and inhabitants of Arab countries.
qaBonusA3: Profits can be withdrawn after reaching $70 in your balance. During the withdrawal process, the bonus returns to the company.
qaBonusA4: Bonus profits are withdrawn exclusively through a USDT wallet, provided that the wallet is being used for the first time for withdrawal from Inzo, i.e., it has not been used for withdrawals previously.
qaBonusA5: The bonus cannot be transferred between accounts.
qaBonusA6: The leverage for the welcome bonus account is 1:100 and it is fixed, it cannot be changed. After a deposit is made, it is changed to 1:500.
qaBonusA7: Some pairs require a high margin to open a trade, and since the leverage for the bonus account is 1:100, the available margin to open a trade on some pairs is insufficient.
qaBonusA8: The bonus is automatically withdrawn from the account if it is not used within 30 days of being granted.
qaTradingA1: The available margin determines the ability to open trades, not the balance or Equity.
qaTradingA2: The company does not charge commissions on trades opened in the Standard, or Stocks accounts, but commissions are imposed on other types of accounts. You can review the commissions by checking the account types on the website.
qaTradingA3: When the margin level reaches 20%, trades are automatically closed from the most losing trade to the least losing trade until the margin level rises again or all trades are closed. This is called a stop out.
qaTradingA4: Take Profit and Stop Loss orders are auxiliary orders set by the trader to determine the amount of profit or loss upon which the trade is automatically closed without direct monitoring by the trader.
qaTradingA5: Our company advises clients not to trade during news releases because liquidity is very high and price movements are very fast, causing price changes within fractions of a second. Orders are executed according to the prices received from the liquidity provider, not according to the numbers shown on the platform.
qaTradingA6: Inzo does not provide trading education courses or recommendations; only our exclusive agents provide them. You can contact them and see what courses and recommendations they offer. Inzo agents link
qaVerificationA1: After visiting the website, Click on "Open a Real Account" Then fill in the required information, agree to the terms and conditions, and click on "Register." Then check your email; you will find a message from us. Please click on it and then click on the "Confirmation URL." After that, a new window will appear. Please click on "Go to the Registration Page" and upload the documents through your profile.
qaVerificationA2: Check your email; you will find a message from us. Please click on it and then click on the "Confirmation URL." After that, a new window will appear. Please click on "Go to the Registration Page" and upload the documents through your  profile.
qaVerificationA3: The documents are too large. Try to show only the edges of the ID, crop part of the background, and reduce the resolution by sending it to a Telegram or WhatsApp account or taking a photo with the WhatsApp camera, saving it, and sending it again. Then, visit our website and upload it through the designated fields.
qaVerificationA4: Account verification takes up to 48 hours.
qaVerificationA5: Any account created using someone else's documents will be rejected.
qaVerificationA6: The account cannot be deleted but will be archived after a year of inactivity.

qaSupportA2_7: After the initial deposit, you can deposit any amount as a secondary deposit, provided it is not less than $50.

qaSupportA3: Within the MT5 platform, go to Account Management. Then click on the "+" sign at the top. Type "Inzo" in the search bar and select the broker name Inzo LLC. Then enter the account number and password, and select the server:
qaSupportA3_2: Server for demo accounts: inzo-Demo Then click on "Register."
qaSupportA4: Authorization failure during login usually occurs due to incorrect login details. If you have verified your account, delete the platform and reinstall it, or clear the cache and log in again with the correct information.
qaSupportA5: When logging into the client portal, click on "Forgot Password" and then enter your email address. You will receive an email; click on it and then click on "Reset Password." You will be redirected to a new page where you can change your password.
qaSupportA6: Log in to the client portal, then click on the three bars at the top right, click on the trading window under deposits and withdrawals, and select "Change Platform Password." Link to change the trading account password on the MetaTrader platform:
qaSupportA7: Upon account verification, an email is sent with two passwords: a password and an investor password. Using the investor password for login opens the account in read-only mode. To avoid this, log in using the primary password.

qaFinanceA6: Transfers between accounts take between 1 minute and 30 minutes.

qaFinanceA8: Security is one of the most important goals that Inzo strives for, so confirming the account holder's identity before the withdrawal process is necessary to keep your account secure.

qaFinanceA13: The withdrawal method must match the deposit method, with an exception allowed after trading a total of 1.00 lots.
qaFinanceA14: You cannot withdraw part of the balance until trading a total of 1.00 lots. The entire balance can be withdrawn after the company’s finance department review

qaAgentsA1: To open an agency with INZO, the agent must have experience in trading, social media accounts, and a trading audience. Contact the relevant department via Telegram at
qaAgentsA2: To transfer your account to a specific agency, log in to the client portal. Then click on the three dots in the top left corner, followed by the question mark. Select "I have a question" and choose "Request to transfer the trading account to a new agent." In the question field, please write your request in this format: "I would like to transfer my account number (mention your account number) to the agency number (mention the agency number). Thank you." Submit the request as shown in the video, and it will be reviewed by the relevant department.
qaAgentsA3: Agents offer training courses in forex trading, recommendation channels, and technical and fundamental analysis.
qaAgentsA4: The agent cannot control the client's account in any way as long as the client does not share their account password.
qaAgentsA5: The agency transfer is only for funded accounts.
qaAgentsA6: No commission is deducted from the account registered under an agency. The agent's commission is directly provided by the company, and the client does not bear any additional costs.
qaAgentsA7: The agent is not a point of contact for deposits and withdrawals. These transactions can only be done through the methods available on the website.
qaDepositBonusA1: Deposit bonuses can only be added to Standard account types.
qaDepositBonusA2: The deposit bonus is 30% of the original deposit amount. This percentage may change during certain promotions or events.
qaDepositBonusA3_1: When a trader transfers funds from a Standard account to another type of account, a portion of the bonus proportional to the transferred amount and the initial bonus percentage will be deducted.
qaDepositBonusA3_2: When a trader transfers funds from one Standard account to another Standard account, a portion of the bonus proportional to the transferred amount and the initial bonus percentage will be transferred.
qaDepositBonusA4: The bonus is tradeable and can be lost but is not withdrawable.
qaDepositBonusA5_1: When a trader withdraws a portion of their balance, a portion of the bonus proportional to the withdrawn amount and the initial bonus percentage will be deducted.
qaDepositBonusA5_2: The entire bonus will be deducted if the entire balance is withdrawn or transferred to an account other than a Standard account.
qaDepositBonusA7: The deposit bonus is granted to residents of all Arab countries except Egypt.
qaDepositBonusA8: If the account is inactive for 3 consecutive months after the bonus is granted, the bonus will be automatically deducted.
qaDepositBonusA9: Abusive strategies are strictly prohibited, and misuse of the bonus is considered a breach of bonus rules. In such cases, the company reserves the right to prevent the trader from receiving bonuses in the future.
